title: Ossona de Mendez–Oum–Wood Conjecture
context_text: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood [19] proved that $f(H) > \mathrm{td}(H) - 1$ for every connected graph $H$, where $f(H)$ is the minimum number of colours $c$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph has a $c$-colouring with every monochromatic component having bounded maximum degree, and $\mathrm{td}(H)$ denotes the tree-depth of $H$. They conjectured that this lower bound is tight. The present paper proves $f(k) \leq \lfloor 3\log_2 k\rfloor$, which is within a factor of 3 of the conjecture for excluded cycles and paths.
attributed_to: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood
paper_contribution: Proves that $f(C_{k+1}) \leq \lfloor 3\log_2 k\rfloor$, which is within a constant factor of 3 of the conjectured value $\mathrm{td}(C_{k+1})-1 = \lceil\log_2(k+1)\rceil$, improving the previous best upper bound from linear in $k$ to $O(\log k)$; the same improvement is obtained for excluded paths $P_{k+1}$.
paper_title: Colourings with Bounded Monochromatic Components in Graphs of Given Circumference
